response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems skills from Sociomantic, where he built async I/O services in DLang handling millions of requests per second on Linux. However, his C++ experience is dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and brief, he lists no Rust, and his recent years are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems programming. He has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience, so while adjacent strengths exist, the core technical requirement of modern C++/Rust proficiency is weakly evidenced.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map contributor
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work in DLang on Linux (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and quality-focused craftsmanship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust listed
No direct routing, map tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/Node product work, away from systems-level engineering

key_factors.factor: Low-latency distributed systems experience on Linux
